findings.title: subscribe.astro URL is still hardcoded in src attribute (line 59)
findings.issue: The img element has src='/images/payment-qr.png' hardcoded. While this is overridden at runtime by loadPaymentQR(), the default src still points to a non-existent file. If JavaScript fails to load, users see a broken image icon instead of the configured placeholder.
findings.suggestion: Change src attribute to empty string or use a data URI placeholder. The JS will override it with the configured URL or hide the element. Alternative: use the same placeholder div structure with a hidden class, not an img with wrong src.
findings.impact: Users with JS disabled or on very slow connections may see a broken image instead of the placeholder message.

findings.title: geocodeAddress returns null silently on HTTP errors (including 429 Rate Limit)
findings.issue: When Nominatim returns a non-200 status (including 429 Too Many Requests from the User-Agent violation), geocodeAddress returns null with no indication to the user that rate limiting occurred. The user sees the same 'Address not found' message.
findings.suggestion: Consider checking for 429 status and providing a more specific message: 'Rate limit exceeded. Please wait before trying again.' Alternatively, add a console.warn for debugging. The current behavior is acceptable for MVP since it degrades gracefully.
findings.impact: Users hitting the Nominatim rate limit won't know why their search failed. They'll see the same generic 'not found' message.
